articles.title: Ubuntu CVE-2026-3888 Bug Lets Attackers Gain Root via systemd Cleanup Timing Exploit
articles.summary: A high-severity security flaw affecting default installations of Ubuntu Desktop versions 24.04 and later could be exploited to escalate privileges to the root level.  Tracked as CVE-2026-3888  (CVSS score: 7.8), the issue could allow an attacker to seize control of a susceptible system.  "This flaw (CVE-2026-3888) allows an unprivileged local attacker to escalate privileges to full root access through the interaction of two standard system components: snap-confine  and systemd-tmpfiles," the Qualys Threat Research Unit (TRU) said . "While the exploit requires a specific time-based window (10–30 days), the resulting impact is a complete compromise of the host system."   The problem, Qualys noted, stems from the unintended interaction of snap-confine, which manages execution environments for snap applications by creating a sandbox, and systemd-tmpfiles, which automatically cleans up temporary files and directories (e.g.,/tmp, /run, and /var/tmp) older than a defined ...
articles.title_fr: Vulnérabilité Ubuntu CVE-2026-3888 Permet aux Attaquants d'Obtenir les Droits Root via une Exploitation de Synchronisation de Nettoyage systemd
articles.summary_fr: Une faille de sécurité critique (CVE-2026-3888, CVSS 7.8) affectant les installations par défaut d'Ubuntu Desktop 24.04+ permet une élévation de privilèges vers le niveau root. Elle résulte d'une interaction inattendue entre `snap-confine` et `systemd-tmpfiles`. Bien que l'exploitation nécessite une fenêtre de temps spécifique (10-30 jours), elle peut entraîner un compromis total du système.

articles.title: Apple Fixes WebKit Vulnerability Enabling Same-Origin Policy Bypass on iOS and macOS
articles.summary: Apple on Tuesday released its first round of Background Security Improvements  to address a security flaw in WebKit that affects iOS, iPadOS, and macOS.  The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20643  (CVSS score: N/A), has been described as a cross-origin issue in WebKit's Navigation API that could be exploited to bypass the same-origin policy when processing maliciously crafted web content.  The flaw affects iOS 26.3.1, iPadOS 26.3.1, macOS 26.3.1, and macOS 26.3.2. It has been addressed with improved input validation in iOS 26.3.1 (a), iPadOS 26.3.1 (a), macOS 26.3.1 (a), and macOS 26.3.2 (a). Security researcher Thomas Espach has been credited with discovering and reporting the shortcoming.  Apple notes  that Background Security Improvements are meant for delivering lightweight security releases for components such as the Safari browser, WebKit framework stack, and other system libraries through smaller, ongoing security patches rather than issuing them as part of larger softwa...
articles.title_fr: Apple Corrige une Vulnérabilité WebKit Permettant le Contournement de la Politique de Même Origine sur iOS et macOS
articles.summary_fr: Apple a publié des améliorations de sécurité pour corriger une faille WebKit (CVE-2026-20643) affectant iOS, iPadOS et macOS. Cette vulnérabilité permettait de contourner la politique de même origine via l'API de navigation de WebKit lors du traitement de contenu web malveillant. La correction a été appliquée via une validation d'entrée améliorée dans les versions (a) des systèmes concernés.

articles.title: Critical Unpatched Telnetd Flaw (CVE-2026-32746) Enables Unauthenticated Root RCE via Port 23
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a critical security flaw impacting the GNU InetUtils telnet daemon (telnetd) that could be exploited by an unauthenticated remote attacker to execute arbitrary code with elevated privileges.  The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-32746 , carries a CVSS score of 9.8 out of 10.0. It has been described as a case of out-of-bounds write in the LINEMODE Set Local Characters (SLC) suboption handler that results in a buffer overflow, ultimately paving the way for code execution.  Israeli cybersecurity company Dream, which discovered and reported the flaw on March 11, 2026, said it affects all versions of the Telnet service implementation through 2.7. A fix for the vulnerability is expected to be available no later than April 1, 2026.   "An unauthenticated remote attacker can exploit this by sending a specially crafted message during the initial connection handshake — before any login prompt appears," Dream said  in an alert. "Successful...
articles.title_fr: Faille Critique Non Corrigée de Telnetd (CVE-2026-32746) Permet l'Exécution de Code à Distance en tant que Root Sans Authentification via le Port 23
articles.summary_fr: Une faille critique (CVE-2026-32746, CVSS 9.8) dans le démon Telnetd de GNU InetUtils permet à un attaquant distant non authentifié d'exécuter du code arbitraire avec des privilèges root. Cette vulnérabilité est une écriture hors limites menant à un débordement de tampon. Elle affecte toutes les versions de Telnet jusqu'à 2.7 et peut être exploitée avant même l'affichage de l'invite de connexion.
